text.title: django-decision-tree Core Rules
text.summary: Six rules placing every type of Django code in exactly one layer.

rule.statement: Views parse HTTP, validate via serializer, call ONE service, build the response. Maximum body length: 25 lines. Forbidden: ORM calls, external API calls, business logic.

rationale.source: Hexagonal architecture; Two Scoops of Django

rule.rationale: Layer purity makes views trivially testable (mock the service, assert HTTP status). Mixed views are a perpetual refactor target.

rule.statement: All DB writes, external API calls, email/SMS sends, queue enqueues live in `services/`. Multi-write functions are decorated `@transaction.atomic`.
rule.rationale: Concentrating side effects in one layer makes them auditable (every write is in one folder) and testable (mock the service in views; assert the side effect in service tests).

rule.statement: Code in `utils/` is pure: same input → same output, no I/O, no DB, no clock, no randomness. Tests use `assert f(x) == y` with zero mocks.

rationale.source: Functional core / imperative shell — Gary Bernhardt

rule.rationale: Pure functions can be tested in three lines. Adding any I/O to utils defeats the layer's purpose.

rule.statement: Dependency direction: views/ → services/ + serializers/. services/ → models/ + utils/ + integrations/. utils/ → stdlib only. NEVER reverse.
rule.rationale: Reverse deps (services importing views, utils importing services) cause circular imports and make layer isolation impossible. The direction is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Celery tasks (`tasks/`) contain exactly one service call. No business logic. `result = service_fn(...); return result`.
rule.rationale: Tasks are an async transport, not a business layer. Logic in the task body cannot be reused synchronously and resists testing.

rule.statement: Third-party SDK wrappers live in `integrations/<vendor>.py`. Inputs are primitive (str, int, dict). NO domain types (no `User`, no `Order`) crossing into integrations.
rule.rationale: Integrations should be reusable across apps and replaceable with mocks in tests. Domain types in the integration layer create coupling that defeats both.
